fields.name: Journal Template Name
fields.tooltip: There are a number of tables and fields that are not currently documented. There is no specific Help for these tables and fields.

fields.name: Line No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the journal line.

fields.name: Item No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the item on the journal line.

fields.name: Entry Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies which type of transaction that the entry is created from.

fields.name: Description
fields.tooltip: Specifies the description of the item on the line.

fields.name: Location Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the location code for the item on the line.

fields.name: Inventory Posting Group
fields.tooltip: There are a number of tables and fields that are not currently documented. There is no specific Help for these tables and fields.

fields.name: Quantity
fields.tooltip: Specifies the quantity of the item in the journal line.

fields.name: Unit Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the amount of the unit in the line of the journal line.

fields.name: Unit Cost
fields.tooltip: Specifies the cost of one unit of the item or resource on the line.

fields.name: Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the amount of the unit in the line of the journal line.

fields.name: Salespers./Purch. Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for the salesperson or purchaser who is linked to the sale or purchase on the journal line.

fields.name: Source Code
fields.tooltip: There are a number of tables and fields that are not currently documented. There is no specific Help for these tables and fields.

fields.name: Shortcut Dimension 1 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 1, which is one of two global dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

fields.name: Shortcut Dimension 2 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 2, which is one of two global dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

fields.name: Indirect Cost %
fields.tooltip: Specifies the percentage of the item's last purchase cost that includes indirect costs, such as freight that is associated with the purchase of the item.

fields.name: Standard Journal Code
fields.tooltip: There are a number of tables and fields that are not currently documented. There is no specific Help for these tables and fields.

fields.name: Reason Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the reason code, a supplementary source code that enables you to trace the entry.

fields.name: Transaction Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the type of transaction that the document represents, for the purpose of reporting to INTRASTAT.

fields.name: Transport Method
fields.tooltip: Specifies the transport method, for the purpose of reporting to INTRASTAT.

fields.name: Country/Region Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the country/region of the address.

fields.name: Qty. (Calculated)
fields.tooltip: There are a number of tables and fields that are not currently documented. There is no specific Help for these tables and fields.

fields.name: Qty. (Phys. Inventory)
fields.tooltip: There are a number of tables and fields that are not currently documented. There is no specific Help for these tables and fields.

fields.name: Phys. Inventory
fields.tooltip: There are a number of tables and fields that are not currently documented. There is no specific Help for these tables and fields.

fields.name: Gen. Bus. Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the vendor's or customer's trade type to link transactions made for this business partner with the appropriate general ledger account according to the general posting setup.

fields.name: Gen. Prod. Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the item's product type to link transactions made for this item with the appropriate general ledger account according to the general posting setup.

fields.name: Entry/Exit Point
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the code of either the port of entry at which the items passed into your country/region or the port of exit through which you shipped the items out of your country/region.
The code is used for reporting to Intrastat and appears in the Intrastat Journal window.

fields.name: Area
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the code for an area at the customer or vendor with which you are trading the items on the line.
The code is used for reporting to Intrastat and appears in the Intrastat Journal window.

fields.name: Transaction Specification
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the transaction specification code that is used for the journal posting.
The code is used for reporting to Intrastat and appears in the Intrastat Journal window.

fields.name: Posting No. Series
fields.tooltip: There are a number of tables and fields that are not currently documented. There is no specific Help for these tables and fields.

fields.name: Dimension Set ID
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a reference to a combination of dimension values. The actual values are stored in the Dimension Set Entry table.


Additional Information


The dimension set ID is not edited directly by users. The value of the field is calculated with the dimension values that users select in the Edit Dimension Set Entries window.


The value 0 represents an empty dimension set.

fields.name: Variant Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the variant of the item on the line.

fields.name: Bin Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the bin where the items are picked or put away.

fields.name: Qty. per Unit of Measure
fields.tooltip: There are a number of tables and fields that are not currently documented. There is no specific Help for these tables and fields.

fields.name: Unit of Measure Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies how each unit of the item or resource is measured, such as in pieces or hours. By default, the value in the Base Unit of Measure field on the item or resource card is inserted.

fields.name: Quantity (Base)
fields.tooltip: There are a number of tables and fields that are not currently documented. There is no specific Help for these tables and fields.

fields.name: Originally Ordered No.
fields.tooltip: There are a number of tables and fields that are not currently documented. There is no specific Help for these tables and fields.

fields.name: Originally Ordered Var. Code
fields.tooltip: There are a number of tables and fields that are not currently documented. There is no specific Help for these tables and fields.

fields.name: Item Category Code
fields.tooltip:
Contains the item category code for the item.
You can use item categories to group default item setup values to make creating new items easier. An item category code specifies the following values:

Default general product posting group.


Default inventory posting group.


Default VAT product posting group.


Default costing method.


To see or select item categories in the Item Category window, click the drop-down arrow in the field.

fields.name: Nonstock
fields.tooltip:
Specifies if the item on the line is a nonstock item.
Nonstock items are items that are not usually kept in inventory.

fields.name: Purchasing Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the purchasing code for the item on the standard item journal line.

fields.name: Value Entry Type
fields.tooltip: There are a number of tables and fields that are not currently documented. There is no specific Help for these tables and fields.

fields.name: Item Charge No.
fields.tooltip: There are a number of tables and fields that are not currently documented. There is no specific Help for these tables and fields.

fields.name: Correction
fields.tooltip:
Specifies if you want the standard item journal line to be posted as a corrective entry.
This field is only used when the journal line acts as a revaluation journal line. For more information, see Correction of Incorrect Posted Documents.

fields.name: Return Reason Code
fields.tooltip: Contains a code that explains why the item is returned.

fields.name: Price Calculation Method
fields.tooltip: Specifies the method that will be used for price calculation in the journal line.

fields.name: Overhead Rate
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the items indirect cost as an absolute amount. The amount is added to the items unit cost to reflect indirect cost or overhead, such as freight and warehouse handling that are associated with the purchase of the item.

Additional Information

The value of the Overhead Rate field on the item card is copied to item journal lines and is used to calculate the value in the Unit Cost field on the standard item journal line.
Note


The Overhead Rate field is related to the Indirect Cost % field on the source item journal where it defines the overhead as a percentage of the value in the Unit Amount field on the standard item journal line.
